preFix.title: Fitting a dishwasher supply and waste connection
preFix.subtitle: Connect a freestanding dishwasher to the kitchen supply and drain
preFix.excerpt: How to connect a dishwasher to the cold water supply and kitchen waste: fitting a compression tee on the cold supply under the sink, connecting the inlet hose, fitting the drain hose to a standpipe or waste trap, and checking for leaks.
preFix.sourceNotes: Collins Complete DIY Manual. Reader's Digest Complete DIY Manual. BS 6700 Water Supply in Buildings.

content.text: Connecting a dishwasher requires two things: a cold water supply taken from the 15 mm pipe under the sink and a drain connection into the kitchen waste system. Both are straightforward using proprietary fittings. The supply connection uses a
tee; the drain connects either to a
or to a spigot outlet on the sink trap.

A 15 mm compression tee with a 3/4 inch BSP outlet (to match the dishwasher inlet hose). PTFE tape. A 15 mm mini pipe cutter. Adjustable spanner, water pump pliers. A 40 mm standpipe kit with a trap, or a sink trap with a spigot outlet for the drain hose. The machine's inlet and outlet hoses, supplied with the appliance.
Step 1, isolate and tap the supply

Turn off the water at the service valve on the cold supply pipe under the sink, or at the mains stopcock. Cut into the 15 mm cold pipe at a convenient point using the mini pipe cutter. Fit the compression tee: slide the cap nut and olive over each cut pipe end, push the pipe fully into the tee body, and tighten each cap nut one-and-a-quarter turns past hand-tight. Wrap PTFE tape onto the BSP male outlet and screw on the appliance inlet hose.

If the sink trap has a spigot outlet: remove the blanking plug, push the drain hose onto the spigot, and fit the hose clip. If there is no spigot: fit a standpipe kit with a 40 mm trap behind the unit, with the standpipe top at least 600 mm above floor level. Loop the drain hose up to the worktop underside before routing it down to the standpipe; this high loop stops back-siphoning.
Step 3, restore supply and check for leaks

Turn the water back on slowly. Check the compression tee fittings and the inlet hose connection for drips. Run a short cycle on the machine and watch the drain hose connection and standpipe during the drain phase. Tighten any weeping compression nut by a quarter-turn at a time until dry; over-tightening shears the olive and makes the joint worse.

initialFindings.message: PATTERN tutorial has no Method/Steps section and no orderedList — sequential instructions missing
